bullets.text: Q2 CR 97.6 — acknowledged as disappointing and below the 96.0 cultural target; 8% premium growth achieved but at unacceptable margin; full rate revision response engaged immediately.

Q1 favorable frequency did not persist; severity trends confirmed 'solidly mid-single digit'; hail, floods, winds, and wildfires elevated property losses; motorcycle exposure elevated by heat and drought.

Rate revision infrastructure shifted to fully responsive mode in Q2; rate adequacy expected to be restored in early Q3; Commercial annual policies slow to reflect rate vs. personal auto monthly cadence.
Written premium grew 8% in Q2 — growth continuing but at unacceptable profitability; rate increases beginning to flow through earned premium as portfolio adjusts toward adequate pricing.

Mobile app released with camera-capture for driver's license and insurance ID — fast quoting expanded from 1 driver/1 vehicle to 3 drivers/3 vehicles; Snapshot 'Test Drive' 30-day free trial launched.

Snapshot 'Test Drive' announced — 30-day free trial allows consumers to experience UBI discounts without abandoning current insurer; removes largest behavioral adoption barrier for skeptical shoppers.
YTD investment total return 3.6% through June; portfolio generating income despite compressed yields; capital position strong enough to absorb loss cost volatility without strategic adjustment.
Conversion rates monitored as rate increases reduce price competitiveness; competitors' actions tracked closely; new business volume intentionally allowed to moderate during rate restoration period.
Claims LAE continuing multi-year improvement trend even as loss costs elevated; efficiency gains in claims operations sustained despite catastrophe volume and severity pressures.
Advertising investment maintained through rate revision period; Flo campaign continued with no strategic pullback; brand building sustained even as pricing competitiveness temporarily reduced.
